title: PM Modi to Campaign in Coimbatore on April 18 for AIADMK-BJP Alliance
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is scheduled to hold a high-profile rally in Coimbatore on April 18 for AIADMK-BJP alliance candidates. Traffic diversions have been announced ahead of the visit, with security tightened across the city.

title: Senthilbalaji Promises 25% Industrial Share for Coimbatore
summary: DMK candidate V. Senthilbalaji (Coimbatore South) promised to raise Coimbatore's share in Tamil Nadu's industrial production to 25%.

title: Vanathi's Family Steps Up Door-to-Door Campaign
summary: BJP candidate Vanathi Srinivasan's husband and sons are leading door-to-door campaigning in Coimbatore North apartment complexes during her hospitalization.

title: 10 DMK Campaign Tents Removed in Coimbatore South
summary: Election Commission acted on AIADMK's complaint and removed 10 DMK campaign tents in Coimbatore South for violating model code of conduct.

title: Postal Voting Begins for Election Duty Personnel
summary: Postal voting has commenced for polling personnel deployed for election duty across Coimbatore.

title: Five Constituencies to Use Two Ballot Units Per Booth
summary: Mettupalayam, Coimbatore North, Thondamuthur, Coimbatore South and Singanallur will use two ballot units per polling station due to high candidate numbers.

title: 183 Candidates in Fray Across 10 Coimbatore Seats
summary: After the final list release, 183 candidates are contesting across 10 assembly seats in Coimbatore. Coimbatore South leads with 31 contenders.

title: Flying Squads Seize ₹5.97 Crore in Cash and Valuables
summary: Flying squads in Coimbatore have seized cash and valuables worth ₹5.97 crore so far. ₹5.06 crore was returned with proper documentation.

title: 100% Voter Turnout Hoardings at Race Course
summary: Hoardings promoting 100% voter turnout have been installed at Race Course in Coimbatore as part of the SVEEP awareness initiative.

title: Home Voting Facility for Senior Citizens Rolled Out
summary: Home voting facility has been rolled out for senior citizens aged 85+ and persons with disabilities across Coimbatore.

title: DMK Functionaries Booked for ₹8,000 Gift Coupons
summary: DMK functionaries were booked for distributing gift coupons worth ₹8,000 in Coimbatore. Annamalai alleged organised vote-buying.

title: ₹10 Crore Worth Jewellery Seized by Poll Squads
summary: Election squads seized gold, diamond, and silver jewellery worth ₹10 crore in Coimbatore during intensified pre-poll checks.

title: Delhi CM Rekha Gupta Campaigns for Vanathi Srinivasan
summary: Delhi CM Rekha Gupta campaigned for BJP candidate Vanathi Srinivasan in Coimbatore North, stressing women's empowerment.

title: EPS Reassures Voters on Delimitation in Coimbatore Rally
summary: Edappadi K. Palaniswami reassured voters on the delimitation bill during a Coimbatore rally, dismissing concerns raised by CM Stalin.

title: P Chidambaram Warns of Reduced Southern Influence
summary: P. Chidambaram addressed reporters in Coimbatore alleging delimitation would suppress South India's voice in Parliament.

title: 450 Nominations Filed; 201 Accepted, 249 Rejected
summary: 450 nominations were filed for 10 constituencies in Coimbatore; 201 accepted and 249 rejected after scrutiny.

title: 18 Independent Candidates in Coimbatore South
summary: Coimbatore South saw 18 independent candidates filing nominations, many from Senthilbalaji's home turf of Karur.

title: Vanathi Srinivasan Declares ₹13 Crore Family Assets
summary: BJP's Vanathi Srinivasan (Coimbatore North) declared family assets worth over ₹13 crore in her election affidavit.

title: Senthilbalaji Declares ₹4.54 Crore in Family Assets
summary: DMK's V. Senthilbalaji and family declared assets worth ₹4.54 crore in his election affidavit.

title: S P Velumani's Family Assets Rise to ₹13.08 Crore
summary: AIADMK's S P Velumani (Thondamuthur) declared family assets increased to ₹13.08 crore.

title: TVK Candidate Asha Senthil's Nomination Rejected
summary: TVK candidate Asha Senthil's nomination was rejected during scrutiny in Coimbatore South.

title: TNAU Extends Holidays to Facilitate Voting
summary: Tamil Nadu Agricultural University has granted extended holidays for students to facilitate voting on April 23.

title: 'Run for Vote' at Race Course Sees 350+ Runners
summary: The 'Run for Vote' event at Race Course saw 350+ runners pledging 100% turnout on polling day.

title: EVM-VVPAT Commissioning with Mock Polls Begins
summary: EVM-VVPAT commissioning began in Coimbatore with mock polls of 1,000 votes in 5% of randomly selected machines.

title: Election Control Room Toll-Free 1800-425-0086 Active
summary: Coimbatore district has a dedicated election control room (toll-free 1800-425-0086) operating 24x7 for complaints.

title: Senthilbalaji Highlights ₹760 Cr for City Roads
summary: Senthilbalaji highlighted the ₹760 crore allocated by DMK government for Coimbatore road improvements during his campaign.

title: Annamalai Predicts Huge NDA Mandate in Western TN
summary: BJP's K. Annamalai campaigned vigorously in Coimbatore predicting a huge NDA mandate in the western region.

title: 8,343 Ballot Units & 5,572 VVPATs Ready
summary: Coimbatore district is ready with 8,343 ballot units and 5,572 VVPATs for the 23 April polling.

title: IT Raids at Textile and Jewellery Shops
summary: IT raids were conducted at textile and jewellery shops in western districts including Coimbatore as part of poll-time enforcement.

title: BJP Performs Silambam in Coimbatore Roadshow
summary: BJP leaders performed silambam during their campaign in Coimbatore to connect with traditional voters.

title: DEO Dances with Tribal Voters in Awareness Drive
summary: The District Election Officer danced with tribal people during a 100% voting awareness campaign in rural Coimbatore.

title: Traffic Curbs Tighten as Campaign Rallies Peak
summary: Traffic diversions have been announced across Coimbatore city as major political events intensify. Heavy vehicle movement has been restricted on key entry routes to manage congestion.

title: Drone Restrictions Imposed Across City
summary: Authorities have imposed drone restrictions across Coimbatore to strengthen security during the final campaign phase ahead of polling.

title: Colleges Modify Schedules for Student Voters
summary: Colleges in Coimbatore are modifying class schedules to encourage student voter participation on April 23. Youth-focused awareness campaigns are gaining strong traction.

title: Civil Organisations Release People-Centric Manifestos
summary: Several civil society organisations in Coimbatore have released people-centric manifestos pushing candidates to commit to local welfare priorities.

title: Sanitation Teams Speed Up Post-Rally Cleanup
summary: Post-event littering has increased after major rallies, with sanitation teams accelerating cleanup efforts at venues across the city.

title: Coimbatore Stays a Critical Electoral Battleground
summary: Political narratives increasingly revolve around state versus central governance as Coimbatore remains one of the most critical electoral battlegrounds in Tamil Nadu.

title: EVM Commissioning Underway in Coimbatore Constituencies
summary: Election officials in Coimbatore have begun the commissioning of EVMs and VVPATs in the presence of candidate agents and observers ahead of the polling day.

title: DMK Intensifies Final-Phase Outreach in Coimbatore
summary: DMK candidates and alliance workers have intensified door-to-door outreach across Coimbatore, focusing on welfare scheme beneficiaries and first-time voters.

title: SVEEP Street Plays Boost Voter Awareness in Coimbatore
summary: SVEEP teams in Coimbatore performed street plays at bus stands and markets to encourage 100% voter turnout on April 23.

title: Flying Squads Intensify Vehicle Checks in Coimbatore
summary: Flying squads in Coimbatore have intensified vehicle checks at major entry points and highway intersections to curb illegal cash movement.

title: Coimbatore Reports Smooth Progress on Postal Ballot Distribution
summary: District election officials confirmed that postal ballot distribution to service voters and senior citizens in Coimbatore is progressing as per schedule.

title: AIADMK Holds Booth-Level Worker Meetings in Coimbatore
summary: AIADMK held booth-level worker meetings across Coimbatore to finalize polling-day strategy and voter mobilization plans.

title: Coimbatore Colleges Host Voter Pledge Programs
summary: Colleges across Coimbatore hosted voter pledge programs for first-time voters, accompanied by mock EVM demonstrations.

title: Coimbatore Static Surveillance Teams Report Daily to Observers
summary: Static surveillance teams in Coimbatore are reporting daily to election observers on cash, liquor, and freebie movement.

title: Strong Rooms Sealed in Coimbatore After Final Randomization
summary: EVM strong rooms across Coimbatore have been sealed under multi-tier security following the final randomization of polling personnel.

title: BJP Highlights Central Schemes During Coimbatore Roadshow
summary: BJP candidates conducted a roadshow in Coimbatore highlighting central government welfare schemes and infrastructure investments.

title: Booth Slip Distribution Crosses Halfway Mark in Coimbatore
summary: Door-to-door booth slip distribution in Coimbatore has crossed the halfway mark, with BLOs targeting full coverage by April 18.

title: MCC Violation Notices Issued in Coimbatore Constituencies
summary: Several model code of conduct violation notices have been issued in Coimbatore constituencies for unauthorised hoardings and campaign material.

title: Coimbatore Collector Reviews Polling Station Readiness
summary: The District Collector held a review meeting with returning officers in Coimbatore to assess polling station readiness, water supply, and accessibility ramps.

title: TVK Cadre Steps Up Door-to-Door Drive in Coimbatore
summary: TVK cadre led by local coordinators stepped up the door-to-door campaign across Coimbatore, distributing pamphlets and party manifestos.

title: Coimbatore Administration Installs Awareness Hoardings
summary: The Coimbatore district administration has installed voter awareness hoardings at major junctions, urging citizens to exercise their franchise.

title: Coimbatore Police Beef Up Security at Sensitive Booths
summary: Coimbatore district police have beefed up security at sensitive booths with paramilitary deployment and additional patrol vehicles.

title: Mock Polls Demonstrated to Candidates in Coimbatore
summary: Mock polls were demonstrated to candidate representatives in Coimbatore as part of EVM transparency measures before the April 23 election.

title: Congress Workers Hold Street-Corner Meetings in Coimbatore
summary: Congress alliance workers held street-corner meetings in Coimbatore to highlight the alliance's promises on education and healthcare.

title: Voter Helpline Sees Spike in Calls from Coimbatore
summary: The state voter helpline has seen a spike in calls from Coimbatore, mostly enquiring about polling station details and voter slip status.

title: Liquor Seizure Reported During Coimbatore Checks
summary: A significant liquor seizure was reported during routine election-related checks in Coimbatore, and the contraband has been impounded.

title: Coimbatore Voter List Final Verification Concluded
summary: Booth-level officers in Coimbatore have concluded the final voter list verification, ensuring electoral roll accuracy ahead of polling.

title: NTK Organises Cycle Rally Across Coimbatore Towns
summary: Naam Tamilar Katchi organised a cycle rally across Coimbatore towns to highlight environmental and identity-based issues ahead of polling.

title: Coimbatore Differently-Abled Voters Get Dedicated Assistance
summary: Differently-abled voters in Coimbatore are receiving dedicated transport and assistance arrangements from the district election office.

title: Income Tax Teams Conduct Discreet Surveys in Coimbatore
summary: Income Tax teams have been conducting discreet surveys in Coimbatore to track unaccounted financial flows linked to the election period.

title: Sector Officers Deployed Across Coimbatore Polling Zones
summary: Sector officers have been deployed across Coimbatore polling zones to monitor turnout, address grievances, and report issues in real time.

title: Star Campaigners Schedule Final Rallies in Coimbatore
summary: Major political parties have scheduled final-phase rallies of star campaigners in Coimbatore as the silent period nears.

title: Social Media Influencers Promote Turnout in Coimbatore
summary: Local social media influencers in Coimbatore are running short-form video campaigns urging youth to vote on April 23.

title: Coimbatore Cybercrime Cell Monitors Election Misinformation
summary: The Coimbatore cybercrime cell is actively monitoring social media platforms for election-related misinformation and hate speech.

title: Coimbatore Election Control Room Operates Round the Clock
summary: The election control room in Coimbatore is functioning round the clock to handle voter complaints, MCC violations, and logistical queries.

title: Women Self-Help Groups Address Coimbatore Candidates
summary: Women self-help group federations in Coimbatore addressed candidates seeking concrete commitments on livelihood and credit access.

title: Coimbatore Senior Citizens Avail Home Voting Facility
summary: Senior citizens above 85 in Coimbatore are availing the home voting facility, with polling teams visiting registered residences.

title: Bank Cash Movement Tracked in Coimbatore
summary: Bank cash movement above the threshold in Coimbatore is being tracked and verified by election expenditure observers.

title: EVM Commissioning Underway in Coimbatore Constituencies
summary: Election officials in Coimbatore have begun the commissioning of EVMs and VVPATs in the presence of candidate agents and observers ahead of the polling day.

title: Trader Associations Submit Memorandum to Coimbatore Candidates
summary: Trader associations submitted a joint memorandum to candidates in Coimbatore seeking GST relief and infrastructure support.

summary: SVEEP teams in Coimbatore performed street plays at bus stands and markets to encourage 100% voter turnout on April 23.

title: Flying Squads Intensify Vehicle Checks in Coimbatore
summary: Flying squads in Coimbatore have intensified vehicle checks at major entry points and highway intersections to curb illegal cash movement.

title: Coimbatore Reports Smooth Progress on Postal Ballot Distribution
summary: District election officials confirmed that postal ballot distribution to service voters and senior citizens in Coimbatore is progressing as per schedule.

title: Coimbatore Farmers Raise Pricing Concerns at Public Meeting
summary: Farmers in Coimbatore raised concerns over crop pricing and procurement at a public meeting attended by alliance candidates.

title: PM Modi's Coimbatore Rally Draws Massive Crowds: 'NDA In, DMK Out'
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi's high-profile rally on April 18 in Coimbatore for the AIADMK-BJP alliance saw huge turnout. Modi declared that Tamil Nadu voters want 'NDA in and DMK out', highlighted the women's reservation bill, and attacked the DMK government on development and delimitation issues.

title: NDA Alliance Intensifies Final-Phase Campaign After Modi Rally
summary: Following PM Modi's massive Coimbatore rally, AIADMK and BJP workers have stepped up door-to-door outreach and roadshows across all 10 constituencies, focusing on women's empowerment and central welfare schemes.

title: Flying Squads Heighten Vigil Post-Modi Rally in Coimbatore
summary: Election flying squads and static surveillance teams have increased vehicle and cash checks across the city following the high-profile NDA rally on April 18 to prevent any model code violations.

title: SVEEP Campaigns Peak as Coimbatore Prepares for April 23 Polling
summary: Voter awareness drives including street plays, college pledges, and social media campaigns by influencers are in full swing to ensure maximum turnout on polling day.

title: EVM Strong Rooms Remain Under Multi-Tier Security
summary: Strong rooms in Coimbatore housing commissioned EVMs and VVPATs continue to be guarded round-the-clock as the district enters the final stretch before April 23 polling.

title: Coimbatore Remains Key Battleground in Final Days of TN Polls
summary: With just four days to go, Coimbatore continues to be a focal point in the Tamil Nadu assembly elections, with both NDA and DMK alliances ramping up efforts following star campaigner visits.
